summary: Fresh-fork smoke test of MOVE GTM platform after UI redesign. Backend: 9/9 pytest cases passed including full HITL lifecycle (create_run -> poll to awaiting_research_approval -> chat scope=research -> delete). Frontend: landing hero/logo/CTA verified; all 5 TopNav testids present; RunStatusPill renders; ResearchSourcesDrawer correctly hidden when no run AND no inline sources on /research; /ideation, /studio, /command-center all render without runtime errors. Zero page errors across all navigations.
critical_code_review_comments: RunContext is in-memory only; activeRun id is persisted to localStorage but the run state is not auto-rehydrated on page refresh/new session. This is currently fine for the agent flow (user creates a fresh run each visit) but may surprise users who expect their last run to reappear. Consider auto-loading the run by id on app mount.

RunStatusPill is fine, but data-testid is not present on the pill element itself; we identified it via text 'No active run' / 'Running'. Adding data-testid="run-status-pill" would harden future tests.
TopNav nav-studio points to /command-center and nav-content points to /studio (swapped semantics with route names). Labels work, but the route naming is confusing for future maintenance.
test_credentials: N/A — app has no user auth
seed_data_creation: Created and deleted 1 test run with query 'Anthropic competitive brief' as part of the lifecycle test (cleaned up).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers health, list_runs, full create→poll→chat→delete lifecycle (~2-3 min runtime due to real agent), and negative cases. UI redesign verified: hero 'Outthink the market. Outmove the competition.' renders, MOVE dark-capsule logo lockup is visible, CTA 'Start building GTM strategy' navigates to /research. ResearchSourcesDrawer behaves correctly — Sources button only shows when a research result view exists, drawer never renders inline. NOTE: RunContext is in-memory so a fresh browser session won't re-hydrate the previous run automatically.
rca of the issue: No bugs found — all backend endpoints and UI flows behave as expected after the redesign.
